title: ditassa ottohuberi
intro: Ditassa ottohuberi is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a climbing woody vine native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its various health benefits. In herbal preparations, Ditassa ottohuberi is often used as a treatment for fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, and is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion.
